title: Studies on Vanadium Salicyl Hydroxamic Acid Complexes ; A 1 :4 Metal-Ligand System
author: SASWATI P. BAG
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Jadavpur University, Calcutta-700032 

  Manuscript Received 2 April 1974 ; revised 25 February 1975, accepted 25 February 1975  

 Vanadium reacts with salicyl hydroxamic acid to form a blue-violet coloured complex with   \(\lambda\)  max at 475 nm at  pH  3.3. The metal-ligand ratio in the complex is 1:4. The step-wise formation constants of the complexes have been evaluated by Yatsimirskii's and Leden's graphical extrapolation methods from extractive photometric data. The values from log K 1 , log K 2 , log K 3  and log K 4  are 3.65±0.05, 2.50±0.05, 2.30±0.05 and 2.15+0.05 respectively
